body.u: I felt as though my adult life had just began when I left school. I thought things would go good, be on the dole for a few weeks and then find myself a job. How wrong you can be, I was more or less on there for six months and out of the blue came a change for the sort of job that I had been looking for. By now I was seventeen and felt that this was the real beginning.
I would be working in an office as a clerk, the money seemed to be alright then.
I would also dream of the day that I would leave the island for good even leaving England.
When being at a certain age you feel that you want to see more of the world and what it had to offer. Friends would say don’t go on living on a dream.

After nearly five years at the same firm I had earntearned myself a higher place which meant more money. My savings was already mounted up and decided to take a holiday. I flew from Gatwick, which then was one of the nearest and clean looking airports.
Three weeks relaxing in the sun, this seemed like luxury being waited on hand and foot by some nice people as well.
There was one who meant a lot to me when I returned home we would write to each other. There was no boyfriend at home and we seemed close.

Work was the only thing that kept my mind from twisting. Hoping that when I got home at night and found that a party was laid on, also with a very special guests. My mum and dad knew how much that he meant to me and sent for him.
Two weeks we had together and off he went home, but before he went home a little box was handed to me- my wishes were being to turn true.
In August we were married and have not {regarted?} since. We visit his family often and stay for some time.
Children were not amoungstamongst my choices, but two soon appear, three years difference.

I finished work now and look upon my children to find there happynesshappiness the way I did. These seem happy memories of years of hard work and dreams which came true. People always say ‘remember the good old day’sdays, they will always be the best’. I think so, the best forty-four years so far I hope they continue!
